title: Sarga 86
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Then, Lakshmana, the best among men, having completely narrated the entire story of Vritra's slaying, proceeded to tell the rest of the tale.” through “Then, all the hosts of gods, accompanied by their preceptors and sages, approached that place where Indra was deluded by fear.” through “Having heard the words of the great gods, the personified sin of Brahman-killing, named Durvasa, chose a different dwelling place in their…”

Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “She declared that with one part of herself, she would dwell for four months of the rainy season in full-watered rivers, causing pride to be…” through “Then the gods replied to him, "O sage, as you say, so let it be; accomplish all that is desired."” through “Having heard these excellent and exceedingly charming words of Lakshmana, the great-souled king, whose valor and energy were equal to Indra's,…”

verses.sanskrit: तदा वृत्रवधं सर्वमखिलेन स लक्ष्मणः । कथयित्वा नरश्रेष्ठः कथाशेषं प्रचक्रमे ।। 7.86.1 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Lakshmana, the best among men, having completely narrated the entire story of Vritra's slaying, proceeded to tell the rest of the tale.

verses.sanskrit: ततो हते महावीर्ये वृत्रे देवभयङ्करे । ब्रह्महत्यावृतः शक्रः सञ्ज्ञां लेभे न वृत्रहा ।। 7.86.2 ।।
verses.translation: After the mighty Vritra, who was a terror to the gods, was slain, Indra, the slayer of Vritra, became enveloped by the sin of Brahmanicide and did not regain his consciousness.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽन्तमाश्रित्य लोकानां नष्टसञ्ज्ञो विचेतनः । कालं तत्रावसत्कञ्चिद्वेष्टमान इवोरगः ।। 7.86.3 ।।
verses.translation: Having lost his senses and consciousness, he (Indra) took refuge at the ends of the worlds and remained there for some time, writhing like a serpent.

verses.sanskrit: अथ नष्टे सहस्राक्षे उद्विग्नमभवज्जगत् । भूमिश्च ध्वस्तसङ्काशा निःस्नेहा शुष्ककानना ।। 7.86.4 ।।
verses.translation: Then, with the disappearance of the thousand-eyed Indra, the world became distressed, and the earth appeared devastated, devoid of moisture, and with dry forests.

verses.sanskrit: निःस्रोतसश्च ते सर्वे तु ह्रदाश्च सरितस्तथा । सङ्क्षोभश्चैव सत्त्वानामनावृष्टिकृतो ऽभवत् ।। 7.86.5 ।।
verses.translation: All those lakes and rivers became dried up without streams, and a great agitation among living beings occurred, caused by the drought.

verses.sanskrit: क्षीयमाणे तु लोके ऽस्मिन्सम्भ्रान्तमनसः सुराः । यदुक्तं विष्णुना पूर्वं तं यज्ञं समुपानयन् ।। 7.86.6 ।।
verses.translation: With bewildered minds, the gods performed that sacrifice which Vishnu had previously instructed, as this world was diminishing.

verses.sanskrit: ततः सर्वे सुरगणाः सोपाध्यायाः सहर्षिभिः । तं देशं समुपाजग्मुर्यत्रेन्द्रो भयमोहितः ।। 7.86.7 ।।
verses.translation: Then, all the hosts of gods, accompanied by their preceptors and sages, approached that place where Indra was deluded by fear.

verses.sanskrit: ते तु दृष्ट्वा सहस्राक्षमावृतं ब्रह्महत्यया । तं पुरस्कृत्य देवेशमश्वमेधमुपाक्रमन् ।। 7.86.8 ।।
verses.translation: Having seen the thousand-eyed Indra enveloped by the sin of Brahmanicide, they honored him and commenced the Ashwamedha sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ऽश्वमेधः सुमहान्महेन्द्रस्य महात्मनः । ववृधे ब्रह्महत्ययाः पावनार्थं नरेश्वर ।। 7.86.9 ।।
verses.translation: O king of men, then the very great Ashwamedha sacrifice of the great-souled Mahendra prospered for the purification from the sin of Brahmanicide.

verses.sanskrit: ततो यज्ञे समाप्ते तु ब्रह्महत्या महात्मनः । अभिगम्याब्रवीद्वाक्यं क्व मे स्थानं विधास्यथ ।। 7.86.10 ।।
verses.translation: When the sacrifice was completed, the personified sin of brahmin-slaughter that clung to the great-souled Indra approached and asked, 'Where will you appoint a dwelling place for me?'

verses.sanskrit: ते तामूचुस्तदा देवास्तुष्टाः प्रीतिसमन्विताः । चतुर्धा विभजात्मानमात्मनैव दुरासदे ।। 7.86.11 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the gods, pleased and filled with affection, said to her (Brahmahatya), "O difficult to approach one, divide yourself into four parts by yourself."

verses.sanskrit: देवानां भाषितं श्रुत्वा ब्रह्महत्या महात्मनाम् । सन्निधौ स्थानमन्यत्र वरयामास दुर्वसा ।। 7.86.12 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the words of the great gods, the personified sin of Brahman-killing, named Durvasa, chose a different dwelling place in their presence.

verses.sanskrit: एकेनांशेन वत्स्यामि पूर्णोदासु नदीषु वै । चतुरो वार्षिकान्मासान्दर्पघ्नी कामवारिणी ।। 7.86.13 ।।
verses.translation: She declared that with one part of herself, she would dwell for four months of the rainy season in full-watered rivers, causing pride to be destroyed and desires to be obstructed.

verses.sanskrit: भूम्यामहं सर्वकालमेकेनांशेन सर्वदा । वसिष्यामि न सन्देहः सत्येनैतद्ब्रवीमि वः ।। 7.86.14 ।।
verses.translation: She further stated that with another part, she would always dwell on the earth at all times, declaring this truth to them without a doubt.

verses.sanskrit: यो ऽयमंशस्तृतीयो मे स्त्रीषु यौवनशालिषु । त्रिरात्रं दर्पपूर्णासु वशिष्ये दर्पघातिनी ।। 7.86.15 ।।
verses.translation: She announced that her third part would dwell for three nights in young women full of pride, acting as a destroyer of their arrogance.

verses.sanskrit: हन्तारो ब्राह्मणान्ये तु मृषापूर्वमदूषकान् । तांश्चतुर्थेन भागेन संश्रयिष्ये सुरर्षभाः ।। 7.86.16 ।।
verses.translation: She concluded by stating, "O best of gods, I shall resort with my fourth part to those who kill innocent Brahmins after falsely accusing them."

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्यूचुस्तां ततो देवा यथा वदसि दुर्वसे । तथा भवतु तत्सर्वं साधयस्व यदीप्सितम् ।। 7.86.17 ।।
verses.translation: Then the gods replied to him, "O sage, as you say, so let it be; accomplish all that is desired."

verses.sanskrit: ततः प्रीत्या ऽन्विता देवाः सहस्राक्षं ववन्दिरे । विज्वरः स च पूतात्मा वासवः समपद्यत ।। 7.86.18 ।।
verses.translation: Then the gods, filled with joy, saluted the thousand-eyed Indra, and Indra himself became free from distress and purified in soul.

verses.sanskrit: प्रशान्तं च जगत्सर्वं सहस्राक्षे प्रतिष्ठिते । यज्ञं चाद्भुतसङ्काशं तदा शक्रो ऽभ्यपूजयत् ।। 7.86.19 ।।
verses.translation: And when the thousand-eyed Indra was re-established, the entire world became peaceful, and then Indra worshipped the wondrous sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: ईदृशो ह्यश्वमेधस्य प्रसादो रघुनन्दन । यजस्व सुमहाभाग हयमेधेन पार्थिव ।। 7.86.20 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, such indeed is the efficacy of the Ashwamedha sacrifice; therefore, O greatly fortunate king, perform the horse sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: इति लक्ष्मणवाक्यमुत्तमं नृपतिरतीव मनोहरं महात्मा । परितोषमवाप हृष्टचेता निशमय्येन्द्रसमानविक्रमौजाः ।। 7.86.21 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard these excellent and exceedingly charming words of Lakshmana, the great-souled king, whose valor and energy were equal to Indra's, felt great satisfaction with a joyful mind. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे षडशीतितमः सर्गः ।। 86 ।। Thus ends the eighty-sixth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
